response.text:
```json { "description": "A vertically stacked list of expandable and collapsible content sections.", "semanticDescription": "The Accordion component displays a list of header items that can be expanded or collapsed to reveal associated content. It is ideal for organizing large amounts of information into a compact, navigable format, such as FAQs, feature lists, or detailed product specifications. Built on Radix UI primitives, it provides robust accessibility features and allows for both single-item and multiple-item expansion patterns.", "tier": "free", "whenToUse": "Use when you need to display multiple sections of content in a limited vertical space, allowing users to focus on specific information without navigating to new pages. It's excellent for FAQs, documentation, or settings panels where content can be grouped thematically.", "whenNotToUse": "Avoid using if all content sections are very short and should be immediately visible, or if the content within each section is critical and requires constant visibility. Also, don't use it for primary navigation unless combined with a distinct navigation pattern.", "patterns": [ "disclosure", "container", "primitive-based", "compound" ], "tokens": [ "color-text-primary", "color-background-surface", "color-border-default", "spacing-md", "spacing-sm", "font-weight-semibold", "border-radius-md" ], "examples": [ "\n \n Is it accessible?\n Yes. It adheres to WAI-ARIA design patterns.\n \n \n Is it styled?\n Yes. It comes with default styling.\n \n", "\n \n First question?\n First answer.\n \n \n Disabled question?\n This content cannot be viewed.\n \n", "\n \n Pricing Information\n \n

Our pricing plans vary based on usage.

\n \n \n \n" ], "relatedComponents": [ "Tabs", "Dialog", "Popover", "Card" ], "a11yNotes": "Ensures proper ARIA attributes (e.g., `aria-expanded`, `aria-controls`, `role=\"heading\"`, `role=\"region\"`) are applied to indicate the state and relationship of components. Manages keyboard navigation, allowing users to navigate between items using arrow keys and toggle expansion with Space or Enter." } ```
